An initialized context object, or `nil` if the object couldn’t be created.

Calling [doc://com.apple.opengles/documentation/OpenGLES/EAGLContext/init(api:)] creates a new [doc://com.apple.opengles/documentation/OpenGLES/EAGLSharegroup] object and attaches it to this context.

# init(api:)

**Initializer**

Initializes and returns a newly allocated rendering context with the specified version of the OpenGL ES rendering API.

## Declaration

```swift
convenience init?(api: EAGLRenderingAPI)
```

## Parameters

- **api**: The desired version of the OpenGL ES rendering API. For legal values, see [doc://com.apple.opengles/documentation/OpenGLES/EAGLRenderingAPI].

## Return Value

An initialized context object, or `nil` if the object couldn’t be created.

## Discussion

To issue OpenGL ES commands to this context, you must first make the context current by calling [doc://com.apple.opengles/documentation/OpenGLES/EAGLContext/setCurrent(_:)].

Calling [doc://com.apple.opengles/documentation/OpenGLES/EAGLContext/init(api:)] creates a new [doc://com.apple.opengles/documentation/OpenGLES/EAGLSharegroup] object and attaches it to this context.

## Related Documentation

- **OpenGL ES Programming Guide**
